name: Calibration
description: Tests whether the system knows what it doesn't know. Should answer questions about discussed topics and abstain from ones never mentioned.

messages.content: I've been really getting into astrophotography lately. Bought a Celestron NexStar 8SE telescope last month. It's amazing what you can capture from the backyard — I got a shot of the Orion Nebula last weekend that actually shows the color.

The NexStar 8SE is a great choice for astrophotography — the GoTo mount makes tracking so much easier. The Orion Nebula is a rewarding target because it's bright enough to show color even in shorter exposures. What camera are you pairing with it?
A ZWO ASI294MC Pro. The cooled sensor really helps with noise on longer exposures. I usually do stacking sessions of about 200 frames at 30 seconds each. Processing in PixInsight.

ZWO's cooled cameras are excellent for deep sky. PixInsight's stacking and post-processing is really the gold standard for astrophotography. 200 frames at 30 seconds gives you a solid hour-plus of integration time.
I've been trying to get more organized with my note-taking. Started with Obsidian about a year ago but just switched to Logseq. The outliner-first approach fits my brain better.
The outliner model in Logseq is quite different from Obsidian's long-form approach. What made you switch — was it the daily journal workflow?
Exactly. The daily journal as the default view is so natural. I just brain-dump everything and then link it later. Obsidian felt like I had to have a structure before I could write anything.
That's a common friction point with Obsidian — the blank page problem. Logseq's journal-first approach lowers the barrier to capture. You can always structure later.

queries.question: What note-taking app are they using now?
What programming languages do they know?
